title: 10 best herbal creams for dental plaque

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with dental plague because its antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause plaque buildup. The menthol in peppermint also has a refreshing effect on the mouth, helping to stimulate saliva production which helps to wash away food particles and neutralize acids. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe gums and reduce inflammation caused by plaque accumulation, making it an effective natural solution for maintaining good oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Peppermint's antibacterial compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help combat bacterial growth on teeth, reducing the formation of plaque and preventing gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth, making it less likely for plaque to accumulate.
Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to plaque formation, keeping teeth and gums healthy.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural antibacterial properties that help combat dental plaque.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are rich in antioxidants and volatile oils that help reduce inflammation and prevent gingivitis, making them effective in preventing dental plaque buildup.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered peppermint with 100 ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the peppermint oil mixture and melt at 80°c for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to 40°c before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer for 10 minutes until it thickens.
Pour the cooled cream into sterile jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Summer savory creams helps with dental plaque because they contain antibacterial properties that target the bacteria responsible for causing plaque buildup. The herbal extracts in these creams, such as tea tree oil and eucalyptus, have natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation and kill off the bacteria. As a result, summer savory creams can help prevent the accumulation of dental plaque, promoting healthier teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Summer savory's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque, preventing the accumulation of harmful biofilms on teeth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in summer savory creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with gum disease, promoting a healthy gum environment and reducing the likelihood of plaque buildup.

Antioxidant properties: Summer savory's antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to the formation of dental plaque, helping to protect teeth and gums from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial compounds, roots help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause dental plaque.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks have antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of plaque-forming bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh satureja hortensis leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Steam the leaves for 30 minutes to enhance their bioactive compounds and then let them cool.
Combine 20% satureja hortensis leaf extract with 80% coconut oil and 10% beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low temperature using a double boiler until the beeswax melts completely after 15 minutes and then stir well.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized containers and let it cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with dental plaque because they contain antiseptic and antibacterial properties that effectively combat the buildup of plaque on teeth. The cream's active ingredients, such as tea tree oil, penetrate deep into the gums and teeth, killing bacteria and preventing the formation of sticky plaque. Regular use of herbal tea tree creams can help maintain a healthy smile by reducing inflammation, bad breath, and gum disease, ultimately promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, helping to reduce the formation of dental plaque and prevent tooth decay.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in herbal tea tree creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of dental plaque buildup.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and redness associated with gum inflammation, which is often caused by poor oral hygiene and dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea tree roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dental plaque.
Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree contain cineole, an essential oil that has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacteria responsible for dental plaque buildup.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100g of cocoa butter, 50g of coconut oil, and 20g of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the cocoa butter, coconut oil, and beeswax mixture by placing it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 10ml of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars and let cool at room temperature for 1 hour before refrigerating overnight.
Once chilled, use a spatula or spoon to scoop out approximately 2g portions of the cream as needed for dental application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural antibacterial properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. When applied topically, these creams can help reduce the biofilm that forms on teeth, preventing the buildup of tartar and promoting healthy gums. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties may also aid in reducing redness and swelling associated with dental plaque.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque and gum disease, reducing inflammation and preventing infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dental plaque and periodontal disease.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum recession.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against gum inflammation and oral infections.
Buds: Containing high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce bacterial growth and prevent plaque formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them properly for at least 2 hours under shade.
Grind 50 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes in a glass container.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the herbal infusion from the solids, discarding the latter.
Blend the infused liquid with 50 grams of petroleum jelly and store it in an airtight jar for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with dental plaque because of its potent antibacterial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for causing plaque buildup. The active compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, penetrate deep into the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and killing off harmful bacteria. Additionally, rosemary's ability to improve blood flow and reduce stress can also help alleviate gingivitis symptoms. By using an herbal rosemary cream as part of a regular oral care routine, individuals can effectively combat dental plaque and maintain healthy, happy smiles.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to kill bacteria responsible for forming dental plaque, reducing inflammation and preventing tooth decay.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with gum disease and gingivitis, promoting healthy gums and a reduced risk of periodontal disease.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, helping to protect against tooth decay, gum recession, and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their antimicrobial properties, making them effective in combating dental plaque and promoting oral health.

Barks: The barks of rosemary plants contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent dental plaque buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them in the shade for 3-4 weeks to preserve their potency.
Combine dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with coconut oil in a ratio of 10:5 in a double boiler, heat gently until it reaches a temperature of 100°f.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to allow the active compounds from the herbs to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax in a ratio of 1:0.25 and heat gently until the wax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 120°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with dental plaque because of its potent antimicrobial properties that effectively combat the buildup of bacteria and other debris on teeth. The cream's active ingredients, such as thujone and camphor, work together to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of tartar, ultimately leading to healthier gums and a stronger smile. By regularly applying sage creams to the affected areas, individuals can enjoy fresher breath, reduced gum irritation, and improved oral hygiene overall.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress on teeth, preventing damage from free radicals that can contribute to plaque formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the mouth, which is a common precursor to plaque buildup and gum disease.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in sage cream help to combat the bacterial growth that causes dental plaque, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation and periodontitis, making them a good addition to creams for dental plaque.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial adhesion, making it effective in treating dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity to yield approximately 20 grams of dried material.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Combine 5 grams of dried salvia officinalis with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the salvia extract is fully incorporated.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and store it in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with dental plaque because of its potent antibacterial properties. The herb contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque and gum disease. When applied topically to the teeth and gums, these compounds help to reduce bacterial colonization, preventing the accumulation of sticky plaque and promoting a healthy mouth environment. By using wild marjoram creams as part of your oral hygiene routine, you can enjoy fresher breath, reduced tartar buildup, and a stronger defense against dental decay.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Wild marjoram contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, preventing its formation and reducing gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of gingivitis and periodontitis.
Antioxidant Activity: Wild marjoram's antioxidant compounds help protect the mouth from free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to plaque buildup, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild marjoram have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with dental plaque.
Seeds: Wild marjoram seeds contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacterial growth and prevent the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves and rinse them with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 50 grams of dried origanum vulgare leaves with 500 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to allow for infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Mix one part of the origanum vulgare extract with one part of beeswax and one part of coconut oil, heating gently until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with dental plaque because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients, specifically the essential oils extracted from the leaves of the Tasmanian Blue Gum tree. These oils have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that work to disrupt the formation of plaque, preventing it from hardening into tartar and reducing the risk of gingivitis and periodontal disease. Additionally, the cream's gentle soothing texture makes it an effective and comfortable way to clean and maintain healthy teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: The cream's antimicrobial property helps to reduce bacterial growth and prevent the formation of biofilms that can lead to dental plaque accumulation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of Tasmanian Blue Gum reduces inflammation and swelling in the gums, which can be caused by dental plaque and gingivitis.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of the cream helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to the development of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with dental plaque.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in preventing the formation and accumulation of dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 250ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to obtain the herbal infusion.
Mix the herbal infusion with 100g of unrefined coconut oil and 50g of beeswax at 60 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers and let it cool at room temperature before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with dental plaque because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque buildup, reducing the risk of cavities and gum disease. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can help calm irritated gums and reduce swelling, promoting healthy oral tissue and a cleaner mouth overall.

Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and redness in the gums and mouth, alleviating symptoms of gingivitis and periodontal disease caused by excessive plaque buildup.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that damage oral tissues and contribute to plaque formation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of dental problems.

Seeds: High in fatty acids and vitamin E, which help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, beneficial for treating gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full bloom and dry them thoroughly using air circulation or dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Combine dried calendula flowers with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler and gently heat the mixture for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids; allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for 1 hour.
Mix the cooled cream with tea tree essential oil at a concentration of 5 drops per tablespoon and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
Apply the calendula cream topically to the teeth and gums twice daily as needed, gently massaging it into the area with a soft cloth for 2 minutes before rinsing with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with dental plaque because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth, making it an effective natural remedy for combating dental plaque. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to destroy existing bacteria and prevent new ones from forming, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of gum disease.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Yarrow's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, including those that contribute to dental plaque and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums and mouth tissues, which can be caused by dental plaque and gum disease.
Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to tooth decay, gum disease, and other oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, making them an effective ingredient in creams for dental plaque.
Buds: Yarrow buds have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that cause dental plaque, making them a valuable addition to medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and dry them in a warm oven for 24 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 2 cups of sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to release essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with dental plaque because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has antimicrobial properties. Berberine has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans. By targeting these bacteria, goldenseal cream can help reduce the accumulation of plaque on teeth and gums, promoting a healthier oral environment and reducing the risk of dental problems like cavities and gum disease. Regular use of goldenseal cream may also help prevent bad breath and improve overall oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound found in goldenseal, has been shown to inhibit the growth and adhesion of oral bacteria responsible for dental plaque formation, thereby reducing plaque buildup.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: The isoquinoline alkaloids present in goldenseal have antimicrobial properties that help kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gums.

Flavonoids: Goldenseal's flavonoid content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to dental plaque formation and gum disease, making it easier for plaque to be removed and preventing further buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce dental plaque and prevent gum disease.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, are rich in berberine and other bioactive compounds that help to combat oral infections and promote healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of dried goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered goldenseal root with 8 ounces of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly until melted and smooth taking about 5 minutes.
Combine the cooled goldenseal infusion with the melted beeswax mixture and stir well to create a consistent texture then pour into glass jars for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque formation. The natural compounds present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce biofilm formation on tooth surfaces, preventing the accumulation of dental plaque. Additionally, licorice creams may also help reduce inflammation and soothe gum tissue, making it an effective complementary treatment for oral health maintenance.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacteria that causes dental caries and plaque buildup.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent further damage caused by dental plaque.
Saponins: Saponins have been found to inhibit the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, making it more difficult for plaque to form and accumulate.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth associated with dental plaque.
Barks: Licorice barks are rich in flavonoids and glycyrrhizin, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat oral bacteria and prevent the formation of dental plaques.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered roots with 1 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a heat-resistant glass bowl and stir for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 180°f to 190°f within 45 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 teaspoons of coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated within 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal them tightly, and store them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours before using as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients, including cinnamaldehyde, work to reduce inflammation in the gums and disrupt the biofilm that leads to plaque formation. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque accumulation, promoting a healthier mouth and reducing the risk of dental problems like gingivitis and periodontitis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Ceylon cinnamon creams, like cinnamic acid, help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can alleviate symptoms of gingivitis and other oral infections.

Antioxidant property: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage tooth enamel and gum tissue, reducing the risk of oral health issues and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, Rhyzomes help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with dental plaque.
Barks: Containing volatile oils and phenolic compounds, Barks exhibit antimicrobial properties that can combat the bacteria responsible for dental plaque buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from local pharmacies or online suppliers.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250ml of distilled water with 20g of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Combine 10g of ground cinnamomum verum bark with the hot oil-water mixture and stir for 15 minutes until well combined.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized glass jars and let it cool to room temperature before sealing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with dental plaque because of its unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to combat oral bacteria. The antibacterial properties in dandelion cream, such as taraxasterol and 6,8-dehydroisoandrosterone, inhibit the growth of plaque-causing bacteria, preventing tartar buildup on teeth. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion reduce gum swelling and redness, making it an effective natural remedy to maintain good oral health and prevent dental problems like gingivitis and cavities.

Seeds: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, these help to reduce plaque formation by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves with 200ml of vodka in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to obtain the infused liquid and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 5 grams of shea butter with the infused liquid in a double boiler until melted.
Pour the mixture into tin containers and let it cool for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The cream's antibacterial agents also inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. Additionally, ginger's ability to stimulate saliva production helps to neutralize acid and remineralize teeth, further preventing the buildup of plaque. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with plaque buildup, making it easier to remove plaque-causing bacteria from teeth and gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh ginger roots, wash them thoroughly with distilled water and dry with clean cloth.
Grind the dried ginger into fine powder using mortar and pestle, store in air-tight container for future use.
Mix 20% of ground ginger powder with 80% of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl, stirring until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low temperature using double boiler or warm water bath, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then transfer to glass jar with tight-fitting lid for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered chamomile with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof glass bowl and soak for 8 hours at room temperature.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has reduced to 100ml and the chamomile extract is fully dissolved after 30 minutes.
Combine the cooled chamomile extract with 100g of unsalted butter and 50g of coconut oil in a mixing bowl and whip until smooth for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 5°c to set for at least 24 hours before use.

Grind 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then mix it with 20 grams of beeswax, heating the mixture gently until the wax is fully incorporated.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacteria growth in the mouth.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a natural oil that helps to soothe gum irritation and reduce pain associated with dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them using a food dehydrator for 24 hours at 60 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing the cloth to extract as much liquid as possible, resulting in approximately 1 cup of infused coconut oil.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/2 cup of shea butter and heat them in a double boiler until melted, then add the 1 cup of infused coconut oil and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into lip balm tubes or small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before use, with an expected shelf life of 6 months when stored properly.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dental plaque are topical remedies that utilize plant-based ingredients to prevent and reduce the accumulation of plaque on teeth. These creams typically contain antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agents that help combat the bacteria responsible for causing plaque buildup, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gums. Examples of herbal creams include those containing tea tree oil, clove extract, and chamomile, which have been shown to effectively inhibit the growth of oral bacteria and alleviate symptoms of gingivitis. By using these creams, individuals can enjoy fresher breath, whiter teeth, and a reduced risk of dental problems, leading to improved overall oral health and confidence in their smile.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dental plaque with herbal creams is to identify effective ingredients such as tea tree oil, eucalyptus oil, and chamomile that exhibit antibacterial properties. Apply the cream to the affected area gently massaging it into the gums for 2-3 minutes. Leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing with warm salt water. This natural treatment helps reduce plaque buildup and prevents gingivitis.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dental plaque that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem have antifungal properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to treat skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis, which share similarities with the inflammation and buildup caused by dental plaque.
